climate.climate_zone: tropical wet & dry (Aw), tropical wet (Ar), steppe or semiarid (Bs), subtropical dry summer (Cs), temperate oceanic (Do), temperate continental (Dc), temperate with humid winters (Df), temperate with dry winters (Dw)
climate.photoperiod: short day (<12 hours), neutral day (12-14 hours), long day (>14 hours)

notes: BRIEF DESCRIPTION A heavy tillering grass reaching a height of 80-150 cm. It develop tillers from bulbils at or below the soil surface. USES The grain is mainly used for malting and as a food source. GROWING PERIOD Perennial. The seeds germinate very quickly after planting, often wth only 20-30 mm of rainfall. COMMON NAMES Bulbous barley, Bulbous wild barley, Orge bulbeuse, Kurram, Kurraym, Subbeil, Qurram, Barley grass, Wild barley, Sha'ir barri. FURTHER INF Scientific synonyms: H. nodosum. Bulbous barley is native of the Mediterranean region. Average dry matter yields for fodder is 0.9-2.6 t/ha. Seed yields may be 200-400 kg/ha.
